description: Deterministic CAM audit rules derived from HP CAM Guide for Fusion CAM add-in.

rules.message: DON'T POST setup is excluded from audit.
Setup name must match 'Op[op#] [#AX]' or an approved support setup name.
Program number is required.
Program number must match O1XXX.
Program comment is required.
Program comment should end with OP#.
Soft jaw setup program comment should end with SOFT JAWS.
DON'T POST setup program comment should end with DON'T POST.
Axis mode is required.
Machine model should be assigned.
Fixture type should be classified.
Work offset is required.
5-axis trunnion must use G59.
Zero-point vise with probing must use G57.
Zero-point vise without probing must use G56.
Left milling vise should use G54.
Right milling vise should use G55.
Axial/radial clearance should be greater than 4 mm.
Op1 setup should be present.
Op2 should be present when the file contains multiple main setups.
Use 'Dovetail Stock Prep' naming when applicable.
Use 'Soft jaw' or 'Soft jaws' naming when applicable.
Use 'DON'T POST' naming for the remove excess stock setup.
Try to limit total tools to fewer than 20.
Operation must have a tool assigned.
Tool number is required.
Tool description is required.
Tool type is required.
Tool preset is required.
Holder should be assigned.
Toolpath must exist.
Cycle time should be available.
Operation name is required.
Operation comment should be present.
Compensation type should be defined.
Standard tool descriptions should keep the .S prefix.
One-off modified tools should remove the .S prefix.
One-off modified tools should be renumbered T999/T998/T997 etc.
Tool number must match the .S prefix number (e.g. .S101 → T101).
Tools without .S prefix must use a reserved number T900–T999.
First toolpath using a one-off tool should contain a note.
Tools smaller than 6 mm should prefer GSK06.
DC holders are only for standard tools.
Shrink fit is only for standard tools.
Holder family should be one of the approved HP holders.
Use FINISH WALL for 2D contours, 2D bores, and 3D contours.
Use FINISH FLOOR for floor finish toolpaths.
HSM presets should be used for roughing only.
Use SLOT sparingly.
Ball end mills should use the Profiling preset for 3D surfacing with <1 mm stock remaining.
Ball end mills used for edge deburring should use the Deburring preset.
Drill operations should define pecking mode according to depth-to-diameter rules.
Thread milling is the preferred method for thread generation.
In aluminum, prefer plunge thread milling.
Chamfer threaded holes before thread milling.
Ano-shifted parts should use [Ano Offset] thread toolpaths.
External edges should be deburred.
Deburring should target approximately 0.125 mm chamfer size.
Tab/web thickness should be defined.
Use a DON’T POST setup for the remove excess stock process.
Programs with more than 50 toolpaths should use folders to keep the browser clean.
All components and sketches should be labeled.
Engraving should use ISO.shx font.
Engraving height should be at least 1 mm.
Target engraving height is 3 mm.
Target engraving depth is 0.0035 in.
Stock sizing should use 0.05 in to bar dimensions.
Stock sizing should use 0.1 in per side to cut dimensions.
Adaptive rough should be the primary roughing method.
Do not use Face to rough material; use it only to finish the face.
Never finish a surface with an adaptive toolpath.
Use 3.5° entry ramps for roughing.
Use 4° ramp taper angles for roughing.
Use Protect on roughing toolpaths that take a long time to generate.
Never rough with 1/2 in end mills on BT30 holders.
Pre-drill holes 10 mm and up before finishing with 2D Bore.
3.5° ramps are standard for bore and ramp toolpaths.
Use either 0.1 mm wall stock for finish pass or cut to size with a spring pass.
Spot drill holes 0.125 in or less with the 1/8 chamfer tool.
Larger holes should be spotted with a 1/4 spot drill.
Drill toolpaths should be labeled with the drill size.
Blind drill and blind tap depth should be checked carefully.
Preferred drill measurement systems: circuit board, metric, fractional, letter, wire.
Thread chamfers should be larger than the major diameter.
When copying toolpaths from another part, update tools to current standard tools.

rules.preferred: Leave as much material holding onto the part as possible for as long as possible.
rules.message: Chase rigidity by leaving support material as long as possible.
Reduce the number of B and C axis rotations to lower cycle time.
Never rub the shank of a tool on material.
Avoid long stickout reduced-shank tools for roughing.
Floor finish operations must not use 'Repeat finishing pass' (nullPass must be false).
Wall finish operations must use 'Repeat finishing pass' (nullPass must be true).
Wall finish operations should not use 'Multiple finishing passes' (doMultipleFinishingPasses should be false).
Use the same tool on all faces of a part before changing tools where practical.
Maximum simultaneous motion is 4-axis, not 5-axis.
Soft jaws should be held a nominal-inch distance apart.
Remaining tab/web thickness should target 0.25 mm.
Soft jaws should have pockets that enclose the part.
Only use tool holders from the HP cloud library.
Document tools needing purchase and number them T999/T998/T997 etc.
Drill descriptions should include diameter and the word 'drill'.
Sketches for toolpaths should be on the top level in the CAM file.
Use parametric height selections whenever possible.
Round soft jaw features should use a circle sketch and 2D bore/contour.
Operations named 'FINISH WALL' must use a preset containing 'FINISH' and 'WALL'.
3D surface finish operations must use a Profiling preset.
Operations named 'FINISH FLOOR' must use a preset containing 'FINISH' and 'FLOOR'.
Operations named 'Engrave' must use a preset containing 'ENGRAVE'.
Operations named 'Profiling'/'Profile' must use a preset containing 'PROFIL'.
Operations named 'Deburr'/'Deburring' must use a preset containing 'DEBURR'.
Operations named 'Chamfer' must use a preset containing 'CHAMFER'.
Pocket clearing operations are not permitted.
